parentQuestion: White matter is found in all of the following locations EXCEPT the ___.

scaffolds.q: In the brain, gray matter is on the outside (cortex) and white matter is inside.
scaffolds.explain: The brain positions gray matter superficially (cortex plus deep nuclei) and white matter internally (fiber tracts). This is opposite of the spinal cord's organization.

scaffolds.q: In the spinal cord, white matter is on the outside and gray matter is inside.
scaffolds.explain: The spinal cord reverses the brain's arrangement: white matter (ascending and descending tracts) forms the outer shell, gray matter (butterfly or H-shaped) occupies the core. Opposite topology, same tissue types.
